vertical: Sales analytics SaaS for mid-market manufacturing — Series B, 160 employees
objective: Land Meridian Analytics as a net-new Conversion customer before the Snowflake Data Cloud Summit window closes. They are actively scoping a HubSpot replacement — hiring signal, frustrated G2 reviews, and conference registration all fired in the last 7 days. The buying committee is the VP Marketing + a newly hired Marketing Data Engineer. Move fast: shelf life ~6 days.
lifecycle_stage: Prospect · New — active buying window, high urgency

segment.name: HubSpot Ceiling → Warehouse-Native MAP Adoption | Meridian Analytics
salesforce_filters: Contact.Title LIKE '%VP Marketing%' OR Contact.Title LIKE '%Marketing Data Engineer%'
warehouse_traits: g2_review_velocity_signal = TRUE AND g2_review_score <= 3 AND g2_review_vendor = 'HubSpot' AND g2_review_age_days <= 7
hiring_signal_role = 'Marketing Data Engineer' AND hiring_signal_keywords_match IN ('Snowflake','Salesforce','marketing attribution') AND hiring_signal_age_days <= 7
conference_registration_event = 'Snowflake Data Cloud Summit 2026' AND conference_registration_contact = 'Jordan Kim' AND conference_registration_age_days <= 10
segment.estimated_size: 1 account / 2 contacts (Jordan Kim – VP Marketing; [MDE_FIRST_NAME] [MDE_LAST_NAME] – Marketing Data Engineer)

touches.timing: Day 0 – send at 08:45 recipient local time, Tuesday or Wednesday preferred; do not send Friday–Sunday
touches.subject: {{contact.first_name}}, your Snowflake data deserves a better MAP than duct tape
touches.body_brief: Open by mirroring the exact language from Jordan's G2 review ('duct tape' + '30% RevOps time on plumbing'). Acknowledge the pain is architectural, not a configuration problem — HubSpot was not built for warehouse-native data flows. Bridge to Conversion: a MAP with a native Snowflake read/write layer that eliminates reverse ETL middleware. Include a 60-second Loom demo link showing Snowflake → Conversion → Salesforce bi-directional sync with zero middleware. Close with a single low-friction CTA: 'See how Meridian's stack would look inside Conversion — 20-min live walkthrough, I'll bring the data model.' P.S. line: 'Saw you're heading to Data Cloud Summit — we're at Booth #412. Happy to show this live on the floor.'
qa_rules: Suppress if Contact.HasOptedOutOfEmail = TRUE or Contact.Unsubscribed__c = TRUE before send
Verify {{signal.g2_review_quote}} resolves to non-null string; block send if null
Verify {{asset.loom_demo_url}} returns HTTP 200 before send; block if dead link
Deduplicate: block send if a touch was already delivered to this contact within the last 24 hours on any channel
Spam-gate: subject line must not trigger HubSpot trademark policy flags — run subject through spam scorer, block if score > 4.0
Validate {{sender.calendar_link}} is a live Calendly/Chili Piper URL returning 200
Confirm send window is Mon–Thu 08:00–17:00 recipient local time; reschedule if outside window
touches.success_metric: Email open within 24 hours OR calendar link click within 48 hours

touches.timing: Day 1 – 12 hours after Step 1 email confirmed delivered; send between 09:00–11:00 recipient local time
touches.subject: LinkedIn DM: Conversion + Snowflake — saw your Data Cloud Summit registration
touches.body_brief: Short, warm LinkedIn DM to Jordan Kim from the assigned AE. Reference the Data Cloud Summit registration organically ('Looking forward to Summit next month — are you going to be on the expo floor?'). Mention Conversion is a sponsor at Booth #412 and that we built our MAP ground-up on Snowflake. Offer to send a one-pager mapping Meridian's current stack (HubSpot + Hightouch/Census + Snowflake) to what a Conversion-native architecture removes. No hard pitch — goal is to open a conversation thread and establish presence before the conference. 300 characters max.
qa_rules: Verify LinkedIn contact profile URL resolves before send; block if profile not found
Confirm Step 1 email was delivered (not bounced) before triggering this touch; suppress if Step 1 hard-bounced
Message length check: body must be <= 300 characters after token injection; block if over limit
Deduplicate: block if a LinkedIn DM was already sent to this contact in the last 7 days
Suppress if contact LinkedIn connection status is not 1st-degree and AE has not sent a connection request; queue connection request first if needed
touches.success_metric: LinkedIn DM read receipt OR reply within 48 hours

touches.timing: Day 2 – create task at 08:00 AE local time; due same day by 17:00
touches.subject: AE Task: Call Jordan Kim + loop in [MDE_NAME] — Meridian buying signal trifecta fired
touches.body_brief: AE task written for the assigned account executive. Summarize the three buying signals with source and age: (1) G2 3-star review quoting 'duct tape' plumbing, 3 days ago; (2) Marketing Data Engineer hire posting requiring Snowflake + out-of-MAP attribution, 5 days ago; (3) Jordan Kim registered for Data Cloud Summit, 8 days ago. Instruct AE to: (a) attempt a warm call to Jordan Kim referencing the Summit and offering to meet at Booth #412; (b) find and connect with the newly hired Marketing Data Engineer on LinkedIn — their Snowflake/attribution background makes them a technical champion; (c) log call outcome in Salesforce Opportunity.Next_Step__c within 2 hours of attempt. Provide suggested call talk-track: lead with the Summit hook, pivot to the attribution-model-outside-MAP pain point from the JD, offer a bespoke data architecture review session.
qa_rules: Verify Opportunity.OwnerId is populated and maps to an active Salesforce user before task creation; block if owner is inactive
Confirm Account.Active_Trial__c = FALSE at task creation time; suppress task if trial has since started
Validate {{contact.phone}} is non-null; flag task with 'Phone Missing — LinkedIn only' if null
Check that Steps 1 and 2 were attempted before creating this task; log warning if either was suppressed
Deduplicate: block if an open AE task for this account was created in the last 48 hours
touches.success_metric: AE logs call attempt in Salesforce within same business day; Opportunity.Next_Step__c updated

touches.timing: Day 3 – send at 09:00 recipient local time if Step 1 email was opened but no meeting booked; suppress if meeting already booked
touches.subject: {{contact.first_name}}, the Meridian stack → Conversion architecture (took 11 minutes to map)
touches.body_brief: Value-forward nurture email addressed to Jordan Kim, cc-awareness for the Marketing Data Engineer persona. Lead with: 'I mapped Meridian's current stack in our sandbox — here's what it looks like.' Include an inline architecture diagram (Snowflake ↔ Conversion ↔ Salesforce) that visually eliminates the Hightouch/Census middleware layer. Quantify the RevOps time savings: 'Teams in your profile recover ~25–30% of RevOps bandwidth in the first 90 days post-migration.' Reference the hiring JD signal naturally: 'Your new Marketing Data Engineer hire will be able to build attribution models directly in SQL against live Conversion events — no ETL layer, no lag.' Second CTA: link to a live sandbox environment pre-seeded with a fictional manufacturing SaaS dataset so Jordan/MDE can self-explore. Tertiary CTA: 'Still going to Summit? We'd love 20 minutes at Booth #412 — I can reserve a private demo slot.'
qa_rules: Suppress entirely if Opportunity.StageName has advanced to 'Meeting Booked' or beyond since Step 1
Suppress if contact replied to Step 1 email with a negative/unsubscribe signal
Verify {{asset.architecture_diagram_url}} returns HTTP 200 and renders inline image; block if broken
Verify {{asset.sandbox_environment_url}} returns HTTP 200 and sandbox is live; block if environment is down
Validate {{signal.reverse_etl_tool_detected}} resolves to 'Hightouch', 'Census', or another named tool — do not send generic placeholder text
Spam-gate: check for image-heavy layout triggering promotional tab classifiers; ensure text-to-image ratio > 60:40
Confirm send is Mon–Thu 08:00–17:00 recipient local time; reschedule otherwise
touches.success_metric: Sandbox environment link click OR calendar booking within 48 hours of send

touches.timing: Day 5 – send at 08:30 recipient local time; this is the final pre-Summit urgency touch; suppress if meeting booked at any prior step
touches.subject: Last call before Summit: reserved a private demo slot at Booth #412 for you, {{contact.first_name}}
touches.body_brief: Short, high-urgency close email. Open with explicit scarcity: 'We have 4 private 20-minute demo slots remaining at our Data Cloud Summit booth (June 11–13, Booth #412) — I've held one under your name through EOD tomorrow.' Reference the full buying-signal context in one tight paragraph: 'You flagged the Snowflake-HubSpot plumbing problem on G2, you're building out a data engineering function that lives outside a traditional MAP, and you're already coming to Summit — this feels like the right moment to see the warehouse-native alternative in person.' Primary CTA: 'Claim your slot — 2 clicks' (deep link to a pre-filled Summit booking form). Secondary CTA: 'Can't do Summit? Here's the async walkthrough instead' (links back to Loom demo from Step 1). Closing line: 'If the timing genuinely isn't right, just reply and I'll stand down — no hard feelings.' Signature includes AE direct mobile.
qa_rules: Hard suppress if Opportunity.StageName = 'Meeting Booked', 'Demo Scheduled', 'Closed Won', or 'Closed Lost'
Hard suppress if contact replied 'not interested', 'unsubscribe', or equivalent at any prior touch — check reply sentiment score < -0.4
Verify {{asset.summit_booking_url}} is live and the booking slot inventory shows >= 1 slot remaining; block send if slots = 0
Verify {{asset.loom_demo_url}} still returns HTTP 200; block if dead
Validate {{sender.mobile_phone}} is non-null and E.164 formatted; block send if missing
Confirm campaign has not exceeded 5-touch send cap for this contact across all channels in this campaign
Spam-gate: 'Last call' and urgency language — run subject through spam scorer; block if score > 3.5
touches.success_metric: Summit demo slot booking OR reply indicating interest/meeting request within 48 hours

optimization_triggers.condition: contact.email_open_count >= 2 AND calendar_link_click = FALSE AND hours_since_step1_send <= 48
optimization_triggers.action: Optimization agent accelerates Step 3 AE task to same day as repeated opens; adds SMS touch to AE task brief flagging 'high-intent lurker — opened twice, not yet clicked'; reallocates Step 4 send to Day 2 instead of Day 3

optimization_triggers.condition: signal.intent_topic_score_map_replacement spikes >= 85 at any point during campaign runtime
optimization_triggers.action: Optimization agent triggers an out-of-sequence in_app_banner touch (if Meridian is already trialing any Conversion feature) AND escalates AE task priority to P0; notifies AE Slack channel with full signal context

optimization_triggers.condition: signal.conference_registration_event = 'Snowflake Data Cloud Summit 2026' AND days_until_conference <= 7 AND Opportunity.StageName NOT IN ('Meeting Booked','Demo Scheduled','Closed Won')
optimization_triggers.action: Optimization agent triggers conference_meet protocol: fast-tracks Step 5 to send immediately (overriding Day 5 schedule), reserves a Summit booth demo slot in the booking system under contact name, and creates a high-priority AE task to hand-deliver a physical one-pager to Jordan Kim at Booth #412 on Day 1 of Summit

Optimization agent immediately suppresses all remaining touches for this contact across all channels; updates Salesforce Contact.Campaign_Suppression_Reason__c = 'Negative reply or unsubscribe during HubSpot Ceiling campaign'; flags opportunity for AE review before any future outreach

optimization_triggers.condition: Step 1 email hard_bounce = TRUE OR Step 1 email delivery_status = 'invalid_address'
optimization_triggers.action: Optimization agent pauses email channel for this contact; re-routes campaign to LinkedIn-first sequence (Step 2 promoted to Step 1 equivalent); triggers AE task to source valid email via ZoomInfo/Clearbit enrichment within 4 hours

optimization_triggers.condition: Opportunity.StageName advances to 'Meeting Booked' or 'Demo Scheduled' at any step
optimization_triggers.action: Optimization agent halts all remaining campaign touches immediately; triggers a new AE task: 'Pre-demo prep — pull Meridian Snowflake schema from warehouse trait and prepare bespoke attribution model demo'; logs campaign as conversion success

pipeline_projection.expected_renewals: Net-new logo close: 65% probability of Closed Won within 21 days of campaign launch, contingent on Summit meeting booked by Day 6. Estimated ACV: $48,000–$72,000 (mid-market MAP contract, 100–300 seat band). If Summit meeting books, close probability increases to 80% within 30 days.
pipeline_projection.ae_efficiency: AE active involvement limited to Step 3 call + Summit booth meeting. Estimated AE time investment: 2.5 hours total across campaign lifecycle (30 min call prep, 20 min call, 20 min LinkedIn research, 60 min Summit demo). All email and LinkedIn DM touches are execution-agent-automated. AE task creation, sequencing, and suppression are fully system-managed — zero manual scheduling overhead.
pipeline_projection.campaign_runtime: 6 days active (Day 0–5), aligned to shelf life of buying signals. Conference hard deadline: Snowflake Data Cloud Summit opens June 11, 2026. Campaign must reach a booked meeting or explicit pass by Day 6 (June 10 latest) to enable Summit activation. Post-Day 6: campaign enters dormant state; optimization agent re-activates only on new intent spike or AE discretion.
pipeline_projection.downside: Downside scenario (35% probability): No email reply, no LinkedIn engagement, no Summit booking by Day 5. In this case: AE logs 'no response' in Opportunity.Next_Step__c; contact enters a 30-day cooling-off suppression window; account is re-queued for next buying signal event (e.g., next G2 review, expanded hiring signal, or post-Summit content engagement). No ACV recognized. Secondary downside: meeting books but deal stalls post-Summit demo — estimated 20% of booked meetings do not advance past discovery. In this sub-scenario, ACV = $0 in current quarter; account enters a 60-day nurture track with a warehouse-native attribution content series.

meta.vertical: Sales analytics SaaS | Mid-market manufacturing | Series B | 160 employees
meta.buying_window: Active — estimated 6-day shelf life from campaign launch
primary_buying_committee: Jordan Kim — VP Marketing (economic buyer, Summit registrant, G2 reviewer)
[MDE_FIRST_NAME] [MDE_LAST_NAME] — Marketing Data Engineer (technical champion, newly hired)
active_buying_signals: G2_REVIEW_VELOCITY: VP Marketing 3-star HubSpot review citing Snowflake plumbing — 3 days ago
HIRING: Marketing Data Engineer JD requiring Snowflake + out-of-MAP attribution — 5 days ago
CONFERENCE_REGISTRATION: Jordan Kim registered for Snowflake Data Cloud Summit 2026 (SF, June 11–13) — 8 days ago
meta.conference_activation: Conversion sponsoring Snowflake Data Cloud Summit 2026 — Booth #412
meta.platform_fit: Every field maps to a Conversion primitive: the segment is Salesforce SOQL plus warehouse trait joins, the touch sequence carries execution / QA / optimization agent assignments, and the whole brief is a typed JSON object delivered by webhook — no human translation step between signal and campaign.
meta.build_time_on_conversion_platform: ~1 day. Segment, agent roles, and suppression rules drop straight into the campaign builder; the signal stack and shelf-life feed the trigger and optimization layers.
meta.what_this_proves: A GTM engineer can compile a buying-window signal stack into a ready-to-run lifecycle campaign — replacing intuition and manual research with a typed, schema-enforced system.

signals.description: VP Marketing left a 3-star G2 review: 'HubSpot Enterprise doesn't talk to our Snowflake warehouse without a lot of duct tape. Our RevOps team spends 30% of their time on plumbing.'
Marketing Data Engineer posted on LinkedIn — JD requires Snowflake, Salesforce, and 'building marketing attribution models from scratch outside of a MAP'
VP Marketing (Jordan Kim) registered for Snowflake Data Cloud Summit 2026 (San Francisco, June 11–13). Conversion is a sponsor with a booth on the expo floor.

why_now.rationale: 3 signals detected in the last 8 days. Buying window closes in ~6 days.
